name: Ground Control
text: Take-and-Hold mirror. Early rounds reward holding the objective majority; from the second battle round, scoring shifts to per-objective control at end of Command Phase, with a linchpin bonus for each central objective controlled.

name: Determined Acquisition
text: Take-and-Hold against a Disruption opponent. Scoring favors aggressive expansion: VP per newly-seized objective each turn, then from the second battle round per-objective control at end of Command Phase with a bonus per objective held in the opponent's territory.

name: Immovable Object
text: Take-and-Hold against a Purge-the-Foe opponent. A central-objective hold pays out each turn, then mid-game the per-objective hold scoring scales up at end of Command Phase. In the final battle round, scoring shifts to end-of-turn — reward for holding through your opponent's response.

name: Unyielding Domination
text: Take-and-Hold against a Priority-Assets opponent. A wide-board hold (3+ objectives) pays out each turn; from the second battle round, holding 2+ is the baseline and beating the opponent's count adds a bonus on top. Seizing the opponent's home objective pays out at the end of the battle.

name: Eliminate and Secure
text: Take-and-Hold against a Reconnaissance opponent. Killing while either you or the victim are standing on an objective pays out at end of turn (you only need one such kill — the two alternatives don't stack). From the second battle round, per-objective control at end of Command Phase becomes the workhorse, plus a bonus for taking a new objective during the turn.

name: Death Trap
text: Disruption against Take-and-Hold. Mine terrain outside your own deployment zone with player actions — each mining pays out, with a bonus when the mined terrain is also an objective. Killing an enemy unit that started the turn in mined terrain pays out separately, and from the second battle round you also score for holding objectives outside your home.

name: Outmaneuver
text: Disruption mirror. Scoring ramps by phase of the game — early rounds pay per-objective at end of turn, mid-game shifts to end of Command Phase with a bigger payout, and the final two rounds return to end of turn with the largest per-objective value. Seizing the opponent's home pays out 10 in any battle round.

name: Delaying Action
text: Disruption against Purge-the-Foe. Take No Prisoners pays per enemy unit you destroy each turn. From the second battle round, holding objectives beyond your home pays out at end of Command Phase, and you score again at end of turn for spreading your hold across both Central and Expansion objectives.

name: Find and Deny
text: Disruption against Priority-Assets. Mark 5 terrain pieces outside your deployment zone at the start of the game; the opponent does actions on those pieces to un-mark them. You score for destroying enemy units in marked terrain, for owning the last remaining marked piece outright (you in, them out), and from the second battle round for holding objectives beyond your home. End of battle pays out again if you still own the last marked piece.

name: Smoke and Mirrors
text: Disruption against Reconnaissance. Player actions on non-home objectives turn them Baited; the opponent (Reconnaissance, playing Observe Enemy) does actions to remove the Baited tag. You score per Baited objective standing at end of turn, with a per-objective bonus when the Baited objective is in the opponent's territory. End of battle pays out a large bonus if 4+ objectives are still Baited.

name: Unstoppable Force
text: Purge-the-Foe against Take-and-Hold. Killing pays out at end of turn (any battle round). From the second battle round, scoring widens: per non-home objective at end of Command Phase, plus a flat bonus for taking a new objective during the turn. End of battle pays out for holding a central objective.

name: Punishment
text: Purge-the-Foe against Disruption. Mark 1-3 enemy units as Doomed (either on objectives, or units that killed a friendly last turn) — destroying or pushing off-board any Doomed unit pays out at end of turn. From the second battle round, holding objectives beyond your home pays at end of Command Phase, with a stacked bonus for owning the objective majority. End of battle pays out for seizing the opponent's home.

name: Meat Grinder
text: Purge-the-Foe mirror. Killing pays out each turn (any battle round). From the second battle round, holding a non-home objective at end of Command Phase pays out, out-trading the opponent's previous-turn kill count adds a bonus, and holding the opponent's home pays out each turn it stays yours.

name: Wrath of the Destroyer
text: Purge-the-Foe against Priority-Assets — the asymmetric counterpart a Purge player reads when the opponent plays Important Connection. Scores for destroying enemy units each turn, then from the second battle round adds objective control beyond your home and an objective-majority bonus, plus a reward for out-trading your opponent turn over turn.

name: Consecration
text: Purge-the-Foe against Reconnaissance. Destroying an enemy unit while the destroying friendly stands on a non-home objective Consecrates that objective. Per-turn scoring tiers on how many objectives are Consecrated (1-2 OR 3+). From the second battle round, classic control scoring stacks alongside. End of battle pays out if the opponent's home is Consecrated.

name: Secure Object
text: Priority-Assets against Take-and-Hold. Performing an action on a non-home objective and killing enemy units standing on no-man's-land objectives each pay out at end of turn. From the second battle round, per-objective hold at end of Command Phase plus a fat-board bonus (3+ objectives) layer the scoring.

name: Recover the Relics
text: Priority-Assets against Disruption. Do actions on central objectives to unmark them; an action completed each turn pays out, with a once-per-game Overwhelming Force bonus and a separate award for being the side that controls the last marked objective. From the second battle round, classic non-home control adds in, and end of battle pays out again for holding the last marked piece.

name: Important Connection
text: Priority-Assets against Purge-the-Foe. Early scoring rewards holding the central objectives, with a per-objective bonus when those central objectives have had an action completed on them. From the second battle round, scoring broadens to any objective beyond your home, with an additional bonus when the held objective is central. Seizing the opponent's home objective pays out at the end of the battle.

name: Sabotage
text: Priority-Assets mirror. Do actions on non-home objectives to Sabotage them — each Sabotage completed pays out, with a per-objective bonus when the Sabotage is in the opponent's territory. From the second battle round, classic non-home control fills out the scoring.

name: Vanguard Operation
text: Priority-Assets against Reconnaissance. Do actions in terrain in the opponent's territory to complete a Vanguard Operation — each turn one such action pays out, with a separate award for killing an enemy unit during the same turn. From the second battle round, non-home control scoring kicks in, and end of battle pays out for seizing the opponent's home.

name: Record-Breaking Mission
text: Reconnaissance against Take-and-Hold. Spread your engaged units across fronts of the battlefield — engaging on 3 fronts pays 3, OR 4 fronts pays 6 (only the higher tier scores). No Prisoners adds a flat VP per enemy unit destroyed. From the second battle round, per-objective control at end of Command Phase fills out the rest.

name: Observe Enemy
text: Reconnaissance against Disruption. Two actions: end moves on Baited objectives to remove the Baited tag, and within 18" of unique enemy units do an action to Spot them. Scoring rewards Spotted enemy units off Baited objectives, classic non-home control from battle round 2 with an objective-majority bonus, and an end-of-battle reward if no Baited objectives remain.

name: Triangulation
text: Reconnaissance against Purge-the-Foe. From battle round 2, one action per turn in your Shooting phase on a non-home objective triangulates it. Scoring tiers on how many objectives are Triangulated at end of turn (1, 2, or 3+ — only the highest tier scores). Classic non-home control fills out, and end of battle pays out for holding 4+ objectives.

name: Find and Eliminate
text: Reconnaissance against Priority-Assets. Holding a central objective and destroying an enemy unit while one of your units stands in terrain each pay out at end of turn. From the second battle round, classic non-home control adds in. End of battle pays out if no enemy units are inside your own territory.

name: Information Gathering
text: Reconnaissance mirror. Battle round 1 pays out for holding a central objective. From the second battle round, classic non-home control plus a per-action-completed scaling reward. End of battle pays out twice — once for 3+ Marked objectives, once if the opponent's home is Marked.

name: Forward Position
text: Establish a forward position. Score the payout at the end of your turn while you hold the opponent's home objective or any expansion objective. If it is drawn in the first battle round, redraw and shuffle this card back.

name: Burden of Trust
text: On draw, assign a friendly unit to guard each objective it controls and remains in range of. Score per guarded objective at the end of the opponent's turn (or the fifth battle round). The fixed approach caps at 9 VP; tactical play caps at 5 VP.

name: Display of Might
text: Dominate the centre with bodies. Score the smaller payout at the end of your turn, or the larger one at the end of the opponent's turn, while you have more non-aircraft, non-battle-shocked units than the enemy wholly within No Man's Land.

name: Assassination
text: Hunt enemy leaders. Fixed play scores per enemy CHARACTER model destroyed each turn, with a bonus for each that had 4 or more wounds. Tactical play instead pays a flat amount for destroying any CHARACTER this turn, or for having destroyed them all by the end of the battle.

name: Centre Ground
text: Own the middle. At the end of your turn, score the lower amount for holding near the centre of the battlefield with no enemy contesting it, or the higher amount for holding it more tightly. Only the better tier scores.

name: Beacon
text: On draw, nominate a friendly beacon unit. At the end of the opponent's turn (or the fifth battle round), score the lower amount while the beacon survives outside your deployment zone, or the higher amount while it survives outside your territory. Only the better tier scores.

name: Outflank
text: Sweep the flanks. At the end of your turn, score the lower amount for non-aircraft, non-battle-shocked units near a battlefield edge outside your territory, or the higher amount for reaching opposite edges. Only the better tier scores.

name: Plunder
text: A unit in a terrain area outside your territory can perform an action in your Shooting phase to plunder it. Score the payout at the end of your turn if a terrain area was plundered. If the Cleanse mission is active for you when this is drawn, redraw and shuffle this card back.

name: Engage on All Fronts
text: Spread your presence across the table. At the end of your turn, score by how many table quarters you have a presence in. Each mode has a three-quarter tier and a higher four-quarter tier, and only the better tier scores; tactical values are higher but cap at 5 VP.

name: Secure No Man's Land
text: Seize the middle ground. Score the payout at the end of your turn while you control two or more objectives within No Man's Land, not counting your home objective.

name: No Prisoners
text: Give no quarter. Score per enemy unit destroyed this turn, evaluated at the end of a turn, up to a ceiling of 5 VP.

name: Bring It Down
text: Bring down the big targets. Score per enemy model with 10 or more wounds destroyed this turn, up to 5 VP. Tactical play scores more per kill than fixed. If there are no such enemy models on the battlefield when drawn, discard and draw a replacement.

name: Overwhelming Force
text: Clear the objectives by force. Score per enemy unit that began the turn within range of one or more objectives and was destroyed, evaluated at the end of your turn. Fixed play caps at 9 VP; tactical play caps at 5 VP.

name: Cleanse
text: Purify objectives with a unit action in your Shooting phase while controlling them. At the end of your turn, score the lower amount for one objective cleansed this turn or the higher amount for two or more. Only the better tier scores. If the Plunder mission is active for you when this is drawn, redraw and shuffle this card back.

name: Behind Enemy Lines
text: Push into the enemy backfield. Score per non-aircraft, non-battle-shocked friendly unit wholly within the opponent's deployment zone, evaluated at the end of your turn. Fixed play caps at 9 VP; tactical play caps at 5 VP. If drawn in the first battle round, redraw and shuffle this card back.

name: Defend Bastion
text: Protect a key position. From the second battle round, at the end of the opponent's turn (or the fifth battle round), score for controlling your home objective, with a bonus if there are no enemy units within your deployment zone. If drawn in the first battle round, redraw and shuffle this card back.

name: Tempting Target
text: PROVISIONAL — values not yet verified against the printed 11e card. At the start of your Command phase nominate one objective marker you do not control; at the end of your turn, score if you control that nominated marker. The fixed and tactical tracks score different flat values. The VP figures here are placeholders pending the official card.

name: A Grievous Blow
text: PROVISIONAL — values not yet verified against the printed 11e card. The 11e renaming of 10e 'Cull the Horde'. Score per destroyed enemy unit that began the battle at 14 or more models, evaluated at the end of a turn, up to a ceiling. If the opponent's army contains no unit of 14 or more models when this card is drawn, redraw and shuffle it back. The per-kill VP and ceiling here are placeholders pending the official card.
